notes.scope: Phase 3.5 Task 4 — InitDiagnostics seam + DiagnosticsState. In-memory write/read seam only; no I/O, no IPC, no network, no persistence in this task.

owaspCoverage.A01_BrokenAccessControl: n/a — single-process in-memory holder. No cross-process surface, no permissions involved. DiagnosticsState is @Singleton in SingletonComponent, reachable only inside the app process via Hilt.
owaspCoverage.A02_CryptographicFailures: n/a — no cryptographic primitives, no secrets, no random.
owaspCoverage.A03_Injection: n/a — no SQL/NoSQL/OS/LDAP/XSS surface. modelName is captured into a data class field; no concatenation into shell, SQL, JSON, or HTML in this task. Downstream rendering into the export .txt (Task 7) will need its own review when that code lands; this task introduces no rendering path.
owaspCoverage.A04_InsecureDesign: Design reviewed against tech-spec Decision 6 (interface seam) and Decision 7 (AtomicReference). Module boundary holds: :core-runtime contains only the pure-Kotlin interface (no Hilt, no Android, no Compose imports — verified by grep on core-runtime/src/main, zero matches for `app.sanctum.machina.diagnostics` and zero for `androidx.compose|androidx.activity`). TAC-7 Compose-free invariant for :core-runtime is preserved. Defence-in-depth contract `end without start = no-op` is correctly implemented via `current?.copy(...)` and explicitly tested.
owaspCoverage.A05_SecurityMisconfiguration: n/a — no manifest changes, no exported components, no headers, no CORS. DiagnosticsModule installs into SingletonComponent only; @Binds carries no scope (correct — scope provided by @Singleton on impl + @InstallIn on module).
owaspCoverage.A06_VulnerableComponents: No new dependencies introduced. Uses already-vendored Hilt 2.57.1 and JDK java.util.concurrent.atomic.AtomicReference. No transitive surface added.
owaspCoverage.A08_DataIntegrity: No deserialisation of untrusted input. No CI/CD changes. AtomicReference.updateAndGet contract is honoured: the lambda is pure (single `current?.copy(...)` expression, no side effects), so CAS retry cannot produce duplicate logging or duplicate writes — matches the JDK contract called out in tasks/4.md `Implementation hints`.
owaspCoverage.A09_LoggingMonitoring: n/a in this task — DiagnosticsState is itself part of the diagnostics surface; ErrorLog wiring is out of scope (no new component name needed; ALLOWED_COMPONENTS list intentionally untouched, matching the Phase 3.5 verification gate at tech-spec line 374).
owaspCoverage.A10_SSRF: n/a — no outbound URL construction, no fetch, no http client surface.

dataClassification.modelName: Storage filename of the on-disk model artifact (e.g. `gemma-3-e2b-it.task`), already present in `chat.model_id`, the bundled `model_allowlist.json`, and on-disk paths. Not user-authored, not a secret, not PII. Safe to render into the user-savable export.
dataClassification.freeRamBytes: Long byte count from `ActivityManager.MemoryInfo.availMem`. Not PII; comparable to a `getprop`-class device telemetry value. Safe to render into the export.
dataClassification.atEpochMs: Unix epoch ms of the init attempt. Not PII. Note for Task 7 review: ensure the export renders this in a stable form (e.g. ISO-8601 UTC) and does not concatenate device timezone identifiers if any future renderer is built — but that is a Task 7 concern, not introduced here.
dataClassification.outcome: Three-state enum (InProgress / Ok / Failed). Not PII.
dataClassification.absentFields: The data model does not carry stack traces, file paths, accelerator strings, native error messages, GL renderer strings, build fingerprints, or user-authored content. This is the right shape for an export-bound surface.

concurrencyAnalysis.casLambdaPurity: `onInitEnd` lambda is `current?.copy(outcome = if (success) Outcome.Ok else Outcome.Failed)` — referentially transparent, no I/O, no logging, no allocation beyond the data-class copy. CAS retry on contention re-runs the lambda safely. No hazard.
concurrencyAnalysis.writeWriteRace: Single writer in production (`DefaultModelRegistry.initialize` under `lifecycleMutex`, per Decision 7) eliminates write-write contention by construction. AtomicReference still gives memory visibility from `Dispatchers.Default` writer to Main reader.
concurrencyAnalysis.readObservability: Reader gets either `null`, a fully-formed `InitSnapshot` from `onInitStart`, or a fully-formed snapshot with flipped `outcome`. The `concurrentWriterReaderNeverSeesMixedState` test asserts that a `(modelName, ram, time)` triple from one attempt never combines with the `outcome` of a different attempt — i.e. no torn reads through the seam.
concurrencyAnalysis.endWithoutStart: Defence-in-depth: `updateAndGet { null?.copy(...) }` returns null and leaves the holder null. Cannot be coerced into a phantom snapshot from a malicious caller invoking `onInitEnd` first. Tested by `onInitEndWithoutStartIsNoop`.

moduleBoundaryIntegrity.TAC7: PASS. `grep -rEn 'androidx.compose|androidx.activity' core-runtime/src/main` → zero matches. The new file `core-runtime/.../InitDiagnostics.kt` is a 14-line pure Kotlin interface with no Android/Hilt/Compose imports.
moduleBoundaryIntegrity.appBackImport: PASS. `grep -rEn 'app.sanctum.machina.diagnostics' core-runtime/src/main` → zero matches. The seam is consumed only by :app (DiagnosticsState, DiagnosticsModule). The reverse import :app → :core-runtime in DiagnosticsState.kt is the legal direction.
moduleBoundaryIntegrity.testSeam: `NoOpInitDiagnostics` lives under `core-runtime/src/test`, not `src/main` — keeps production graph clean while letting Task 6 registry tests avoid pulling :app deps.

notes.exportRiskForTask7: Data model is minimal and intentional. No path strings, no native error messages, no stack frames, no settings values, no chat content, no auth material — i.e. nothing in the snapshot would expose a secret if it later lands on disk via SAF export. The Task 7 renderer should still be reviewed in its own pass for: (a) stable timestamp formatting that does not leak timezone/locale identifiers beyond what the user sees, (b) no accidental concatenation of `Throwable.message` from any future field addition, (c) ALLOWED_COMPONENTS list still untouched at 14 entries (tech-spec gate 374). None of these are blockers for Task 4.
notes.hardcodedSecretsScan: Scanned all six files for API_KEY / SECRET / PASSWORD / TOKEN / private-key / connection-string / base64-credential patterns. Zero hits. Test-fixture constants are numeric (`FREE_RAM_A = 4_000_000_000L`, `AT_A = 1_700_000_000_000L`) and the literal `"model-x"` is a synthetic label, not a credential.
